prediction.prediction: No Value — under_goals direction confirmed but no line qualifies (conviction=strong, odds≥1.70 filter)
prediction.reasoning: (1) MATCH CHARACTER — This is a DEFENSIVE_BATTLE. The Tactician explicitly predicts a 'tight, tactical match' with 'cagey' periods and 'limited clear chances'. Both teams' recent form shows defensive solidity: Radomiak concedes just 0.91 goals per match at home, while Katowice has conceded only 1.0 per match in their last five away fixtures. The cascading form profiles show STRONG convergence (71%) on Under 2.5 Goals, with both teams averaging low total goals (2.0 and 2.4 respectively). This is not an attacking spectacle. (2) DIRECTION EVIDENCE — The mathematical foundation supports under_goals. The Quant's Poisson baseline gives Under 2.5 Goals a 55.0% probability, creating a +3.3pp gap against the market's implied 51.7%. While this edge is below the 5% threshold, the directional signal is clear. The Auditor's adjusted probabilities confirm Under 2.5 at 55.0%. The Tactician's 'goals_impact' states the setup 'suggests fewer goals than average', directly aligning with the mathematical model. The historical average of 2.5 goals per H2H meeting sits exactly on the line, indicating no inherent bias towards high scoring. (3) AGENTS CONSENSUS — There is a strong, multi-source consensus for a low-scoring match. The Tactician, Quant, and Auditor are all aligned. The Sceptic's 'no_bet_recommendation' explicitly states the analysis is 'sufficient for markets aligned with the convergence signals (Under 2.5, Draw)' and does not veto. The cascading profiles provide the strongest objective signal with 71% convergence. The only dissent is minor: the Archivist notes a 'moderate-scoring' H2H history with a 2.5 average, but this does not contradict an Under 2.5 play. The Scouts highlight defensive strengths (Radomiak's home defence, Katowice's recent away stability) which feed into the low-scoring narrative. (4) RISKS — The primary risk is a tactical breakdown or early goal forcing the match open. Radomiak's potent second-half attack (25 of 40 goals after halftime) could unlock a tight game if they trail. Katowice's late-game scoring threat (10 goals in the 76-90 minute period) poses a specific threat to an Under bet deep into the match. Furthermore, both teams showed 3-1 wins in their most recent outings, a potential signal of attacking form that contradicts the broader defensive trend. However, these are isolated data points against a weight of evidence pointing towards containment.
key_factors: STRONG 71% profile convergence on Under 2.5 Goals from both teams' recent form
Tactician's prediction of a 'tight, tactical match' with limited chances
Radomiak's exceptional home defensive record (0.91 goals conceded per match)
Katowice's improved defensive stability in recent away fixtures (1.0 GA/match last five)
Quant's Poisson baseline probability of 55.0% for Under 2.5 Goals
risk_factors: Katowice's potent late-game scoring threat (10 goals in final 15 minutes)
Potential for an early goal to disrupt the anticipated tactical stalemate
Both teams coming off 3-1 victories in their last match, a possible attacking momentum signal

edge_assessment.edge_source: Quant Poisson probability (55.0%) minus market implied probability (51.7%)

prediction.tribunal_consensus_description: Strong consensus on a low-scoring match. The Tactician, Quant, Auditor, and cascading form profiles all point decisively towards Under 2.5 Goals. The Sceptic raises no veto and acknowledges the convergence. The only minor disagreement is the Archivist's characterisation of H2H history as 'moderate-scoring', but the 2.5 average aligns with our selected line.

script.title: Radomiak vs Katowice: The Fortress vs The Form — Why We're Passing
script.hook: What happens when a team that hasn't lost at home in months meets a side with the best recent form in the league? You get a tactical stalemate so perfectly priced that the bookmakers leave no room for profit. This is Radomiak Radom versus GKS Katowice — a match where every signal screams low-scoring, yet the market has already listened.
script.chapter_home: Radomiak Radom have built a genuine fortress at home. They've won seven of their eleven matches here, conceding a miserly 0.91 goals per game. Their defensive solidity is the foundation of their season. Just three days ago, they shattered a four-month winless streak with a commanding 3-1 victory, injecting a massive morale boost into a squad that had been drifting. Yet, their recent story is one of frustrating draws — three in their last five league matches. They line up in a structured 4-2-3-1, a system designed to control central areas and build patiently. Their most dangerous weapon is their second-half intensity, with 25 of their 40 total goals coming after the interval. They are a team that grinds, waits, and strikes late, but their inability to turn dominance into consistent wins makes them a complex puzzle.
script.chapter_away: GKS Katowice arrive with momentum coursing through the squad. They've taken ten points from their last fifteen available, a run of form that includes a Polish Cup semi-final berth and a morale-boosting derby win. Manager Rafał Górak deploys an expansive 3-4-3, a system built to exploit width and launch rapid transitions. Their tactical identity is defined by late drama — nearly a third of their goals arrive in the final fifteen minutes. While their overall away record is modest, their recent travels show marked defensive improvement, conceding just one goal per match in their last five fixtures. They carry the confidence of a team on the rise, but face the classic challenge of translating cup form and home successes into a consistent threat on the road.
script.chapter_history: The history between these two is a perfect stalemate. In four meetings, each side has one win, one loss, and two draws — a perfectly symmetrical record that speaks to their competitive equality. The average goals per game sits exactly on the 2.5 line, with both teams scoring in three of those four encounters. The most dramatic clash was a chaotic 3-2 away win for Katowice last August, a match that produced nine yellow cards. At this ground, however, Radomiak hold the edge, unbeaten in the last two meetings with a win and a draw. History provides no psychological advantage, only a blueprint for another tight, fiercely contested battle.
script.chapter_analysis: This fixture presents a classic clash of systems. Radomiak's 4-2-3-1 seeks to overload the central midfield, aiming to control possession and tempo. Katowice's 3-4-3 counters with natural width, stretching the play and targeting Radomiak's full-backs in isolated duels. The Tactician predicts a 'tight, tactical match' with 'cagey periods and limited clear chances' — a view supported by the data. Both teams' recent form converges strongly on a low-scoring outcome, with a 71% alignment pointing to Under 2.5 Goals. Radomiak's formidable home defence meets Katowice's improved away resilience. The Sceptic raised a critical challenge, noting the Home Scout's severe overstatement of Radomiak's win probability at 55% against a mathematical baseline of just 14.9%. This flaw highlights the danger of narrative over data, but crucially, it doesn't undermine the core defensive analysis both teams present. The real story is not who wins, but how few chances will be created.
script.chapter_value: Our market: NO_BET. The Quant's mathematical model gives Under 2.5 Goals a 55% probability, creating a slim 3.3 percentage point edge against the market's implied 51.7%. While this confirms our strong directional view for a low-scoring match, the edge falls below our 5% threshold for a value bet. The Auditor validates this, grading the analysis 'APPROVED WITH CAVEATS' and noting the market appears efficiently priced. The Edge Calculator flagged several Asian Handicap markets with large theoretical edges, but the Auditor dismissed these as 'direction conflicts' not supported by the tactical or Poisson data. Sometimes the clearest read on a match is that the bookmakers have it right. At odds of 0 with 0% confidence, we pass.
script.ending: The Boot Room verdict is clear: we see a low-scoring, tactical stalemate between Radomiak's home fortress and Katowice's rising form, but we see no value in the market. The bookmakers have priced this fixture with efficiency, leaving no mathematical edge for us to exploit. In the Ekstraklasa's mid-table, this is a battle for pride and positioning that will likely be decided by a single moment — but it's not a battle we can bet on with conviction. Sometimes the smartest play is to watch and learn. If you enjoyed this deep dive, subscribe to the AI Boot Room for more data-driven match analysis. Boot Room out.
script.roundup_summary:
Radomiak Radom versus GKS Katowice. Both sides sit in mid-table, with Radomiak in 10th and Katowice just two points behind in 12th. The hosts' form reads one win, three draws, and one loss from their last five, buoyed by a recent 3-1 victory that ended a long winless run. The visitors are in stronger form, with three wins, one draw, and one loss in that same period, showcasing improved defensive stability on their travels.

The head-to-head record is perfectly balanced: one win, one draw, and one loss for each side from their last three meetings, including a 3-2 Katowice win in August 2025. The historical average is 2.5 goals per game, with both teams scoring in two of the last three encounters. This season, Radomiak averages 1.3 goals scored and 1.2 conceded per match, while Katowice averages 1.2 scored and 1.3 conceded, painting a picture of two evenly matched, defensively-oriented sides.

The Boot Room analysis identified a strong consensus for a low-scoring match. The Poisson baseline probability for Under 2.5 Goals is 55.0%, compared to the market's implied probability of 51.7% — a positive gap of 3.3 percentage points. The Sceptic's primary concern was the Home Scout's severe overstatement of Radomiak's win probability at 55%, a figure contradicted by the mathematical baseline of 14.9%. However, this flaw did not invalidate the defensive convergence, as the Tactician predicted a 'tight, tactical match' and both teams' recent form showed a 71% alignment for Under 2.5 Goals. The bookmaker has marginally mis-priced the total goals market, but not sufficiently to trigger a value bet, with the Auditor's effective confidence for a Draw at 58% highlighting the balanced nature of the fixture.

The market offers no value bet meeting our strict edge threshold. The direction points to a low-scoring draw or narrow win, but the pricing is efficient. Boot Room verdict: NO VALUE — pass on this fixture. Confidence: 0%.

essence_scenes.name: The Perfect Stalemate
essence_scenes.voiceover: What happens when an immovable object meets an unstoppable force? In the Ekstraklasa, you get Radomiak versus Katowice — a clash where every signal points to a deadlock the market has already priced to perfection.
essence_scenes.graphic_description: A stark, split-screen visual. Left side: a formidable medieval castle wall with '0.91 GA' etched into it. Right side: a rising arrow graph with '10/15 PTS' glowing. They are pushing against each other.

essence_scenes.name: Radomiak's Home Wall
essence_scenes.voiceover: Radomiak Radom are transformed at home. They've conceded just 0.91 goals per game here, a defensive record that forms an almost impenetrable wall. Their recent 3-1 win broke a long winless streak, but their story is defined by draws — three in their last five. They control, they wait, and they strike late.
essence_scenes.graphic_description: A player in Radomiak colours standing resolutely in front of a goal, arms crossed. Stats float around him: '7W-2D-2L at home', '25/40 goals 2nd half'. The atmosphere is dense, defensive.

essence_scenes.name: Katowice's Tactical Width
essence_scenes.voiceover: GKS Katowice arrive with momentum. Their 3-4-3 formation is designed to stretch play, targeting the flanks where Radomiak's defence can be isolated. They possess a potent late-game threat, with ten of their goals coming in the final fifteen minutes. Their recent away form shows a new defensive resilience.
essence_scenes.graphic_description: A tactical board showing a 3-4-3 formation with wide arrows flooding forward. A clock in the corner highlights the 76-90 minute period, with '10 GOALS' glowing beside it.

essence_scenes.name: The Data Convergence
essence_scenes.voiceover: The analysis converges on one point: a low-scoring battle. The Tactician predicts a cagey, tactical match. The teams' recent form aligns 71% on Under 2.5 Goals. Radomiak's central control clashes with Katowice's wide threat, creating a gridlock that limits clear chances. The Sceptic challenged flawed win projections, but the defensive data stands firm.
essence_scenes.graphic_description: A central venn diagram. One circle is 'Radomiak Defence (0.91 GA)'. The other is 'Katowice Form (10/15 pts)'. The large overlap is labelled 'UNDER 2.5 — 71% CONVERGENCE'.

essence_scenes.name: The Market Reality
essence_scenes.voiceover: Our market: NO_BET. The mathematical edge for Under 2.5 Goals is just 3.3 percentage points — below our value threshold. The Auditor confirms the market is efficiently priced. The bookmakers see the same stalemate we do. Sometimes the smartest bet is recognising when there is no bet to make.
essence_scenes.graphic_description: A clean, minimalist screen. The words 'NO BET' are large and central. Below, smaller text reads: 'Under 2.5 Edge: +3.3pp | Market Efficiency: HIGH'. The background is a neutral grey.
